donation_funds.name: General Club Fund
donation_funds.description: Supports the club’s day-to-day running costs, hall hire, and utilities.

donation_funds.name: Youth Projects Fund
donation_funds.description: Funds outreach, youth workshops, and resources for young railway enthusiasts.

donation_funds.name: Layout & Infrastructure Fund
donation_funds.description: Dedicated to maintaining and upgrading club layouts, tools, and exhibition infrastructure.

donation_funds.name: Heritage Preservation Fund
donation_funds.description: Helps restore and preserve vintage models, control systems, and club archives.

events.name: Spring Open Day & Mini Exhibition
events.description: A relaxed open day with club layouts running, a small sales stand, and guided tours of the workshop.
events.venue_address: Riverside Community Hall, Station Road, Brookford BK1 2LM
events.highlight_text: Next Saturday: Drop in to see the club layouts in action – free entry.

events.name: Digital Control (DCC) Clinic Evening
events.description: An evening of short talks and demonstrations on DCC wiring, decoder fitting, and troubleshooting.
events.venue_address: Riverside Community Hall, Station Road, Brookford BK1 2LM
events.highlight_text: Hands-on help with your DCC-equipped locos and layouts.

events.name: Spring Exhibition & Model Railway Show 2026
events.description: Our main annual exhibition featuring visiting layouts in all major scales, traders, demonstrations, and a dedicated kids’ zone.
events.venue_address: Brookford Exhibition Centre, River Way, Brookford BK3 4EX
events.highlight_text: The club’s flagship two-day show with over 25 layouts and trade stands.

events.name: Kids’ Model Railway Fun Day
events.description: Family-friendly day with hands-on shunting puzzles, simple scenery building, and short introductory talks for children.
events.venue_address: Riverside Community Hall, Station Road, Brookford BK1 2LM
events.highlight_text: Perfect for younger visitors trying model railways for the first time.

events.name: Summer Model Railway Show 2026
events.description: A mid-year exhibition focusing on modern-image layouts, DCC sound, and railway operations.
events.venue_address: Brookford Exhibition Centre, River Way, Brookford BK3 4EX
events.highlight_text: Modern traction, DCC sound, and operations-focused layouts.

events.name: Heritage & Steam Weekend 2026
events.description: A themed weekend celebrating the steam era with period layouts, talks on railway history, and a display of vintage models.
events.venue_address: Brookford Transport Museum, Depot Lane, Brookford BK5 7TR
events.highlight_text: Step back in time with steam-era layouts and talks.

events.name: Winter Members’ Meet 2026
events.description: A low-key members’ running day with invited guests and a short AGM during the lunch break.
events.venue_address: Riverside Community Hall, Station Road, Brookford BK1 2LM
events.highlight_text: Thank you to everyone who attended our winter meet.

events.name: Autumn Exhibition 2025
events.description: Last year’s main public exhibition featuring guest layouts and specialist traders.
events.venue_address: Brookford Exhibition Centre, River Way, Brookford BK3 4EX
events.highlight_text: Our 2025 show attracted over 2,000 visitors across the weekend.

layouts.title: Riverside Branch (1950s N Gauge)
layouts.description: A compact N scale branch line set in the late 1950s, featuring steam tank engines shuttling between a riverside terminus and the main line.

layouts.title: Pennine Summit Steam
layouts.description: N scale double-track main line climbing over a bleak summit, with heavy steam-hauled freight and express trains.

layouts.title: Harbour Sidings 1938
layouts.description: A busy N gauge harbour scene in the late 1930s, with short steam locomotives shunting wagons along the quayside.

layouts.title: Valley Milk Run
layouts.description: Single-line N scale rural branch with early BR steam, focusing on milk and parcels traffic in the early 1950s.

layouts.title: Depot in the Dales
layouts.description: An N scale steam-era MPD with coaling stage, turntable, and roundhouse, ideal for displaying a large collection of locomotives.

layouts.title: Canal Wharf Test Track
layouts.description: A simple N gauge steam micro-layout built as a test track with basic scenery.

layouts.title: Airport Parkway
layouts.description: Modern-image N scale station with EMUs and DMUs serving a busy airport interchange.

layouts.title: Mountain Pass Logging
layouts.description: HO scale US logging line with geared steam locomotives winding through dense forest.

layouts.title: Citylink Intermodal
layouts.description: Contemporary HO container terminal with long freight trains and working cranes.

layouts.title: Brookford Central
layouts.description: OO gauge representation of the club’s home town station, with stock from steam through to present day.

layouts.title: Goods Yard in O Scale
layouts.description: Detailed O scale shunting layout focusing on wagon-load freight operations in the late 1960s.

layouts.title: Riverside Garden Railway
layouts.description: G scale outdoor garden railway with live steam running around ponds and planting.

membership_plans.name: Adult Annual Membership
membership_plans.description: Standard adult membership for one year, including access to club running nights, newsletters, and discounted exhibition tickets.

membership_plans.name: Adult Annual Supporting Membership
membership_plans.description: As standard adult membership, plus a built-in donation to support youth projects and exhibition costs.

membership_plans.name: Family Annual Membership
membership_plans.description: Membership for up to two adults and three children at the same address, ideal for families getting into the hobby together.

membership_plans.name: Junior Annual Membership
membership_plans.description: Discounted membership for under-18s, including supervised access to youth running sessions and workshops.

parking_options.name: On-site Exhibition Centre Car Park
parking_options.address: Brookford Exhibition Centre, River Way, Brookford BK3 4EX
parking_options.notes: Pay-and-display car park directly adjacent to the main entrance. Height restriction of 2.1m.

parking_options.name: Riverside Retail Park Car Park
parking_options.address: Riverside Retail Park, Wharf Street, Brookford BK3 5QP
parking_options.notes: Free parking for exhibition visitors at weekends; 5–7 minutes’ walk along signed pedestrian route.

parking_options.name: Brookford Station Multistorey
parking_options.address: Brookford Station Car Park, Station Road, Brookford BK1 2ST
parking_options.notes: Large multistorey next to the railway station with day and weekend rates.

parking_options.name: Transport Museum Overflow Car Park
parking_options.notes: Usually available during the Heritage & Steam Weekend; unpaved surface.

parking_options.name: North Avenue On-Street Parking
parking_options.notes: Free on-street parking after 6pm and all day Sunday; check local signage.

parking_options.name: Accessible Blue Badge Bays
parking_options.address: Front entrance lay-by, Brookford Exhibition Centre, River Way, Brookford BK3 4EX
parking_options.notes: Limited number of free bays reserved for Blue Badge holders only, next to the main doors.

policy_articles.title: Ticket & Membership Terms and Conditions
policy_articles.content: This policy sets out the terms and conditions for purchasing exhibition tickets and applying for club membership. Tickets are non-transferable and valid only for the date and entry slot selected at the time of purchase. Concession and child tickets may require proof of eligibility on entry. Annual memberships run for 12 months from the date of approval and renewals are billed at the prevailing rate. The club reserves the right to refuse entry or membership in cases of abusive behaviour. Full details, including refund and cancellation rules for tickets and memberships, are provided on this page.

policy_articles.title: Privacy & Cookies Policy
policy_articles.content: We take your privacy seriously. This policy explains how we collect, use, and store your personal data when you browse this website, purchase tickets, join the club, or make a donation. We only collect information necessary to provide our services and will not sell your data to third parties. Cookies are used to keep track of your session, preferences, and shopping cart. You can manage your cookie preferences via your browser settings. For full details of your rights under data protection law, including the right to access, correct, or delete your data, please read this page carefully.

policy_articles.title: Accessibility Statement
policy_articles.content: The club is committed to making both our venue and this website accessible to as many people as possible. The main exhibition centre offers step-free access, accessible toilets, and reserved Blue Badge parking bays near the entrance. Most layouts are displayed at a height suitable for wheelchair users and children. On the website, we aim to follow recognised accessibility guidelines, provide good colour contrast, and ensure all images have meaningful alternative text. If you encounter any accessibility barriers, please contact us so we can review and improve the experience.

workshops.title: Junior Railway Builders – Beginner
workshops.description: Hands-on beginner workshop where children build a small scenic board and learn the basics of track laying and wiring.
workshops.location: Riverside Model Railway Clubrooms, Workshop Room 1

workshops.title: Family Introduction to Model Railways
workshops.description: A relaxed session for families covering scales, control systems, and how to get started at home.
workshops.location: Brookford Exhibition Centre – Seminar Room A

workshops.title: Scenic Modelling Basics
workshops.description: Learn how to create realistic grass, trees, rock faces, and water features using commonly available materials.
workshops.location: Riverside Model Railway Clubrooms, Workshop Room 2

workshops.title: Digital Control (DCC) for Beginners
workshops.description: An introductory workshop explaining how DCC works, how to choose a system, and how to fit basic decoders.

workshops.title: Advanced DCC Sound & Automation
workshops.description: For experienced modellers looking to add high-quality sound, consisting, and computer control to their layouts.
workshops.location: Riverside Model Railway Clubrooms, Control Lab

workshops.title: Realistic Weathering Techniques
workshops.description: Intermediate-level session covering airbrush, powders, and washes to weather locomotives and rolling stock.

workshops.title: Kids’ Shunting Puzzle Session
workshops.description: Short, fun sessions where children can operate a simple shunting layout and learn basic railway operations.

workshops.title: Heritage Modelling Secrets
workshops.description: A talk-and-demo style workshop about modelling the steam era accurately, including research tips and kit-bashing.

talks.title: Building Compact N Scale Layouts
talks.description: Tips and tricks for planning and building N scale layouts in small spaces, including baseboards, curves, and reliable wiring.

talks.title: Steam Era Operation on a Modern Layout
talks.description: How to run prototypical steam-era timetables and freight flows on layouts designed for DCC and contemporary control systems.

talks.title: DCC Sound for Beginners
talks.description: An introduction to choosing and fitting DCC sound decoders, with live demonstrations of sound-equipped locomotives.

talks.title: Weathering Masterclass Live Demo
talks.description: Watch a wagon and a diesel loco transformed with airbrush and powders, including Q&A with the demonstrator.

talks.title: Kids' Layout Design Q&A
talks.description: Informal Q&A session for younger visitors and their parents about building their first layout at home.
talks.location: Brookford Exhibition Centre  Kids' Zone Stage

talks.title: Layout Photography Basics
talks.description: Learn how to photograph your models and layouts for social media and magazines using simple equipment.

talks.title: History of the Brookford Branch Line
talks.description: Illustrated talk on the real railway that inspired many of the club's layouts, from opening to closure.

talks.title: Steam Legends of the 1930s
talks.description: A look at iconic express steam locomotives and how to model them accurately in smaller scales.

talks.title: Preserving Vintage Models
talks.description: Advice on caring for and restoring early tinplate and brass models without destroying their value.

talks.title: Welcome to the Brookford Model Railway Club
talks.description: Short introductory talk about the club's activities, layouts, and how to get involved.

talks.title: Evening DCC Troubleshooting Clinic
talks.description: Bring your own locos and controllers for hands-on help with common DCC issues.
